name: Mountain biking loop in Sierra de Grazalema
name_original: Circular en BTT por Sierra de Grazalema
description:
The north of the Sierra de Grazalema is a paradise of lush forests and picturesque white villages. Here, the pure air and the crystal-clear waters of the rivers create a serene and charming environment, perfect for disconnecting and enjoying nature in its purest state.

The circular itinerary that we propose begins in Zahara, a charming town next to the Zahara reservoir, with a medieval castle that watches over it. You can arrive in your own vehicle and use one of the public parking spaces available. If you need supplies for the Route, do not hesitate to visit the local shops while you walk through the alleys of this charming place.

The Route runs along tracks, paths and some sections of road. It is a very steep area, which will surprise you at every bend with a different postcard. 

You ascend up to 930 meters above sea level and some peaks, over 1500 meters, will witness your adventure. Be sure to check the weather before leaving home to wear appropriate clothing. Summers are hot and we recommend avoiding the hottest hours. A grove or on the banks of a stream or lake will be a good option to rest.
region_intro: Mountain biking around Zahara de la Sierra offers diverse terrain within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park, a region characterized by dramatic limestone massifs, craggy peaks, and deep canyons. The area features technical sections, steep climbs, and thrilling descents, alongside paths winding through olive groves and ancient Abies pinsapo forests. Riders can expect a variety of surfaces, including red dirt over limestone and rocky sections. The landscape is further defined by the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir and the iconic white villages. Best mountain bike trails around Zahara The most popular mountain bike trail is Mountain biking loop in Sierra de Grazalema, a difficult 52.8 miles (85.0 km) trail that takes 7 hours 51 minutes to complete. This route features significant elevation gains within the protected natural park. Another top favourite among local mountain bikers is Grazalema village – Puerto del Boyar Viewpoint loop from Zahara, a difficult 49.9 miles (80.3 km) path. This route offers panoramic views of the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir and surrounding white villages. Local mountain bikers also love the Castillo Tunnel – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Algodonales, a 38.7 miles (62.2 km) trail leading through tunnels and viaducts along a former railway line, often completed in about 6 hours 7 minutes. Mountain biking around Zahara is defined by limestone massifs, olive groves, and views of the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir. The network offers options for various ability levels, from challenging technical routes to smoother greenways. The routes in Zahara are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from more than 50 reviews. More than 600 mountain bikers have used komoot to explore Zahara's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many mountain bike trails are available around Zahara?
A: There are nearly 60 mountain bike trails around Zahara de la Sierra, offering a wide range of experiences within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park. These routes cater to various skill levels, from moderate to difficult.

Q: Are permits required for mountain biking in the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park?
A: Yes, some specific routes or areas within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park may require permits, especially those that traverse more sensitive ecological zones or protected areas like the 'Pinsapos' forests. It's advisable to check with the park authorities for the latest regulations before planning your ride.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on mountain bike trails around Zahara?
A: The terrain around Zahara is diverse and often challenging. Riders can expect a variety of surfaces, including red dirt over limestone, rocky sections, and paths winding through olive groves. Some routes feature technical sections, steep climbs, and thrilling descents, while others, like sections of the Vía Verde de la Sierra, offer smoother, hard-packed gravel.

Q: Are there any family-friendly mountain bike trails in Zahara de la Sierra?
A: While many trails around Zahara are classified as difficult, there are options suitable for families. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/19321783" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo Tunnel – Vía Verde de la Sierra loop from Algodonales&lt;/a&gt;, for example, follows a former railway line and offers a more accessible and scenic ride through tunnels and viaducts, suitable for various fitness levels.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the mountain bike trails in Zahara?
A: Regulations regarding dogs on trails within the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park can vary. While some trails may permit dogs on a leash, others, especially those in protected wildlife areas, might have restrictions. It's best to verify specific trail rules with local park information centers to ensure compliance and safety for your pet and local wildlife.

Q: Where can I find parking when mountain biking in Zahara de la Sierra?
A: Zahara de la Sierra, being a popular white village, offers various parking options, particularly near the village center or at the start points of popular routes. Look for designated parking areas within the village or at trailheads. Some routes, like those starting from Algodonales, will have parking available in those towns.

Q: What are some notable natural attractions or viewpoints I can see while mountain biking?
A: Many routes offer breathtaking views and pass by significant natural attractions. You can find stunning vistas of the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir, such as from the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4723003" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of the Zahara-El Gastor reservoir&lt;/a&gt; highlight. Other routes may lead you past unique geological formations, including limestone massifs and craggy peaks, or even through ancient 'Pinsapos' forests. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazalema village – Puerto del Boyar Viewpoint loop from Zahara&lt;/a&gt; offers panoramic views of the reservoir and surrounding white villages.

Q: Are there mountain bike routes that offer a circular experience?
A: Yes, many mountain bike trails around Zahara are designed as loops, allowing you to start and end in the same location. Examples include the challenging &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1494770274/mountain-biking-loop-in-sierra-de-grazalema" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mountain biking loop in Sierra de Grazalema&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazalema village – Puerto del Boyar Viewpoint loop from Zahara&lt;/a&gt;, both offering extensive circular rides.

Q: What is the best time of year for mountain biking in Zahara?
A: The spring and autumn months are generally considered the best for mountain biking in Zahara. During these seasons, the temperatures are milder, and the natural landscape is at its most vibrant. Summers can be very hot, making early morning or late afternoon rides preferable, while winters can bring cooler temperatures and occasional rain.

Q: What do other mountain bikers say about the trails in Zahara?
A: The mountain bike trails around Zahara are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from over 50 reviews. Riders often praise the diverse terrain, the challenging climbs, and the breathtaking views of the Sierra de Grazalema Natural Park and the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir.

Q: Are there any places to eat or stay near the mountain bike trails in Zahara?
A: Zahara de la Sierra is a charming white village with various options for accommodation, cafes, and restaurants. You'll find places to refuel and relax within the village itself, and other nearby white villages also offer amenities. Many routes start or pass close to these villages, making it convenient to plan your ride around a meal or an overnight stay.

Q: How challenging are the mountain bike trails around Zahara generally?
A: The mountain bike trails around Zahara are predominantly challenging. Out of nearly 60 routes, 25 are classified as difficult, and 33 as moderate. These often feature significant elevation gains, technical sections, and require a good level of fitness. There is only one trail classified as easy, making it a destination more suited for experienced riders or those seeking a robust challenge.

Q: Is it possible to reach the mountain biking areas around Zahara using public transport?
A: Public transport options to Zahara de la Sierra are limited, typically involving bus services from larger towns in the Cádiz province. While it's possible to reach the village, accessing specific trailheads directly by public transport might be challenging. Many visitors prefer to use a car to get to the starting points of the mountain bike routes.

Q: Can I find routes that offer views of the Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir?
A: Absolutely. The Zahara-El Gastor Reservoir is a prominent feature of the landscape, and many mountain bike routes are designed to showcase its stunning turquoise waters. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31815866" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Grazalema village – Puerto del Boyar Viewpoint loop from Zahara&lt;/a&gt; offers spectacular panoramic views of the reservoir and the surrounding area.
tips: Villaluenga del Rosario, nestled in the heart of the Sierra de Grazalema natural park in Cádiz, is a charming Andalusian town known for its cobbled streets, white houses and panoramic views of the surrounding mountains. If you pass by here, it is worth a visit.
It is a very quiet road that connects the towns of Benaocaz and Villaluenga del Rosario, it has unbeatable views of the valley and the surrounding mountains.
The Roman road between Benaocaz and Ubrique is an ancient path that is today used by hikers and mountain bikers. Along this route, remains of the original infrastructure can still be seen.

The Sierra de Grazalema, located in southern Spain, is an ideal destination for outdoor activities such as hiking and mountain biking. Its humid climate makes it one of the rainiest areas of the Iberian Peninsula, favoring lush vegetation and rich fauna.
